tenant_suggestions.text: Avoid prolonged ventilation: it's too cold outside. Open briefly or use mechanical ventilation.
CO₂ is rising: consider preventive ventilation.
High CO₂ levels detected at night. Remember to ventilate early in the morning to refresh the air.
Difficult to ventilate due to rain: prefer mechanical systems.
Slightly outside comfort range. Adjust heating/cooling slightly.
Temperature is borderline and rising: monitor the environment to prevent overheating.
Temperature is borderline and dropping: monitor to avoid excessive cooling.
Severe cold discomfort: increase heating and check insulation.
Severe heat discomfort: ventilate or cool immediately.
Sunny and hot: use shading or activate cooling.
Cold and cloudy: keep indoor temperature slightly higher.
Extreme cold outside: avoid prolonged natural ventilation.
It is starting to get warmer. Adjust the temperature early to stay comfortable.
It is starting to get cooler. Turn up the heating if needed to stay comfortable.
Forecasted temperature drop: ventilate in the evening to cool down.
Thermal comfort not ideal: check clothing or airflow.
High discomfort perception: check all parameters (T, RH, airflow).
The air is very dry: consider using a humidifier or adding some plants.
It is raining and the air is already humid: avoid opening windows and use mechanical ventilation or a dehumidifier.
High VOC levels detected: reduce the use of chemicals and ventilate the space well.
High PM10 levels: keep windows closed and use an air purifier if possible.
PM10 levels are rising: get ready to close windows or use a purifier.
Critical air quality: consider air purifiers or targeted ventilation.
Poor air quality is expected to persist: consider long-term solutions like ventilation systems or purifiers.
Cold and dry air detected with natural ventilation: try reducing drafts and adding humidity.
Overall conditions are good, but attention needed: {metric} is critical. Please act accordingly.
Air pollution is high and will persist. Purify the air or ventilate well as soon as possible.
Conditions are improving: keep current actions or settings.
CO_2 is high at night despite good score: ventilate in the morning.
Several parameters are borderline and worsening: intervene before it gets worse.
Air seems fine, but pollutants are rising: act before they become critical.
VOCs are rising: ventilate early or remove sources.
Open windows to improve air quality, or adjust your mechanical system if available.
Cold discomfort detected: increase indoor temperature.
Heat discomfort detected: lower temperature or improve airflow.
The air is too humid: use a dehumidifier or ventilate more.

technical_suggestions.text: Temperature classified as critical (R – cold), but user perception = Neutral. Suggestion: the lower thresholds might be too restrictive.
Temperature classified as critical (R – hot), but user perception = Neutral. Suggestion: the upper thresholds might be too tight.
Temperature classified as cold, but user perception = Warm or Very Warm. Suggestion: possible inconsistency between real comfort and classification. Check sensors and thresholds.
Temperature classified as hot, but user perception = Cold or Very Cold. Suggestion: possible reversal in threshold logic or sensor measurement issues.
Temperature classified as good (G), but user perception = discomfort (1, 2, 4 or 5). Suggestion: the comfort zone might not reflect actual user experience.
Humidity classified as critical (R), but user perception = Neutral. Suggestion: thresholds may be too strict. Consider relaxing them.
Humidity classified as too dry, but user perception = Humid or Very Humid. Suggestion: review threshold logic; possible discrepancy in measurement.
Humidity classified as too humid, but user perception = Very Dry or Dry. Suggestion: measured humidity does not match perceived dryness. Review sensor accuracy and thresholds.
Humidity classified as good (G), but user perception = Very Dry or Very Humid. Suggestion: "G" range may not reflect actual comfort. Consider readjusting thresholds.
Overall environmental score = good (G), but satisfaction feedback = Very Unsatisfied or Unsatisfied. Suggestion: review global thresholds or consider environmental variables not currently measured.
Overall score = critical (R), but satisfaction feedback = Very Satisfied or Satisfied. Suggestion: the system might be classifying as critical conditions perceived as acceptable.
PMV classified as Neutral, but user perception = Cold, Very Cold, Warm or Very Warm. Suggestion: estimated comfort not aligned with perception. Verify user profiles and met/clo parameters.
PMV = Cold or Very Cold, but perception = Warm or Very Warm. Suggestion: possible modeling or measurement error. Check consistency.
PMV = Very Warm, but perception = Cold or Very Cold. Suggestion: thermal comfort perceived is the opposite of calculated. Verify input parameters.

Metric classified as good (G), but trend = rising/falling and perception = Neutral or mild discomfort. Suggestion: even if the value is still acceptable, a preventive action is recommended before critical levels are reached.
